title: ETL: Fetch External API into Local Table
description: ARCHETYPE: etl_pipeline. Shape: scheduled job fetches data from a third-party API and transforms + writes to a local table. The CORE is `call api 'URL'` + data transform + save. NOT a batch_job (batch is pure local cleanup). NOT a dashboard (no UI). Backend-only. Schedule: every 1 hour, fetch weather from 'https://api.weather-mock.example.com/current?city=SF', extract { temp, humidity, conditions }, save to WeatherReadings table with fetched_at timestamp. Plus ONE GET /api/readings endpoint so tests can verify rows landed. No auth, no pages, no other endpoints.
skeleton:
build for javascript backend

create a WeatherReadings table:
  temp (number)
  humidity (number)
  conditions
  fetched_at_date, auto

# Your scheduled ETL block + GET /api/readings endpoint here.
# Use: every 1 hour: then `call api '...'` then save fields to WeatherReadings.
